back_translation: A Shiggaion of David, which he sang to the LORD concerning Cush, a Benjamite.
O LORD my God, I take refuge in You; save me from all who pursue me, and deliver me.
lest he tear my life like a lion, ripping it to pieces with no one to rescue.
O LORD my God, if I have done this, if there is injustice in my hands,
if I have repaid evil to the one at peace with me, or plundered my foe without cause,
then let the enemy pursue my life and overtake it; let him trample my life to the ground and lay my honor in the dust. Selah
Arise, O LORD, in Your anger; rise up against the fury of my foes; awake for me—You have ordained judgment.
Let the assembly of peoples surround You; take Your seat over them on high.
The LORD judges the peoples; judge me, O LORD, according to my righteousness and my integrity.
Let the evil of the wicked come to an end, but establish the righteous, O righteous God who searches hearts and inmost being.
My shield is with God, who saves the upright in heart.
God is a righteous judge, a God who feels indignation every day.
If one does not repent, He will sharpen His sword; He has bent and strung His bow and made it ready.
He has prepared His deadly weapons; He makes His arrows into fiery shafts.
Behold, the wicked conceives evil; he is pregnant with trouble and gives birth to falsehood.
He digs a pit and hollows it out, and falls into the hole he has made.
His trouble returns upon his own head, and his violence comes down on his own skull.
I will give thanks to the LORD for His righteousness, and sing praise to the name of the LORD Most High.
